records.ety: [OE. dalien, dailien; cf. Icel. pylja to talk, G. dallen, dalen, dahlen, to trifle, talk nonsense, OSw. tule a droll or funny man; or AS. dol foolish, E. dull.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dallied (dl"ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Dallying.]

defs: To waste time in effeminate or voluptuous pleasures, or in idleness; to fool away time; to delay unnecessarily; to tarry; to trifle.
quotes: We have trifled too long already; it is madness to dally any longer.
We have put off God, and dallied with his grace.

defs: To interchange caresses, especially with one of the opposite sex; to use fondling; to wanton; to sport.
quotes: Not dallying with a brace of courtesans.
Our aerie . . . dallies with the wind.

defs: To delay unnecessarily; to while away.
quotes: Dallying off the time with often skirmishes.
